offer.what: Free weekly local newsletter for Mobile, AL residents + paid advertising packages for local businesses (directory listings, banner ads, business spotlights, event listings, Facebook posts)
offer.coreOutcome: Subscribers stay effortlessly informed about everything happening in Mobile, AL without hunting across platforms; advertisers reach a hyper-local engaged audience of 500+ newsletter subscribers and 10,000+ Facebook community members
offer.pricePoint: Free for subscribers; advertising from $25 (single event listing) to $325/month (Gold package); Business Spotlight $95 one-time; Bronze $95/mo, Silver $175/mo, Gold $325/mo
offer.commitment: No commitment for subscribers (free, unsubscribe anytime); month-to-month or per-4-issue packages for advertisers — no long-term contract mentioned
leadMagnets: Free business directory listing (add your business at no cost)

audience.icp: Dual ICP: (1) Mobile, AL residents aged 25–65 who want curated local news delivered to their inbox; (2) Mobile, AL small business owners who want hyper-local brand visibility and community reach at accessible price points
painPoints: Hard to find all local Mobile news in one place without hunting across platforms
National and regional media ignores the hyperlocal stories that actually affect daily life
Local businesses struggle to reach a genuinely local audience affordably — Google and Facebook ads lack true neighborhood targeting at small scale
Missing community events, restaurant openings, and neighborhood developments until after they happen
Small business owners feel invisible to the community they serve
segments: Newsletter subscribers — Mobile, AL residents (consumer side, free)
Paying advertisers — local SMBs wanting community reach and brand awareness (revenue side)
Free directory listers — businesses in the directory but not yet buying ad packages (warm leads for upsell)

salesCycle.length: Subscribers: same-day self-serve. Advertisers: days to 1–2 weeks (inquiry form → 24-hour response → verbal or email close)
salesCycle.trial: No formal free trial for advertising. The free newsletter itself allows prospective advertisers to evaluate content quality and audience fit before committing. Free directory listing serves as a low-friction entry point.
salesCycle.closeMechanism: Subscribers: self-serve web form (email + name). Advertisers: contact form submission → team responds within 24 hours → close by email or phone
salesCycle.topObjection: Advertisers: list size of 500+ subscribers raises ROI questions vs. cost. Subscribers: fear of spam or irrelevant content drowning their inbox.

ahaMoment: Subscribers: opening the first newsletter and spotting a local event, restaurant, or neighborhood story they'd have completely missed otherwise — that 'I almost didn't know about this' moment. Advertisers: receiving an inbound inquiry, call, or mention from a customer who saw the Business Spotlight or newsletter ad.

retention.recurring: Advertisers on Bronze/Silver/Gold monthly packages; subscribers on a free weekly send cadence — retention = continued opens and clicks
retention.expansion: Bronze ($95) → Silver ($175) → Gold ($325) package upgrades; one-off add-ons (Business Spotlight $95, Event Listing $25, Facebook Post $50) create natural upsell moments between billing cycles
retention.churnRisk: Subscribers unsubscribe if content feels too ad-heavy or stops being locally relevant. Advertisers churn if they cannot attribute ROI — small list size makes this a live objection that must be addressed proactively with analytics and success stories.

lifecycleGaps: Welcome & Onboarding: new subscriber likely receives only a confirmation email — no sequence reinforcing the value or driving first engagement
Lead-Magnet Nurture: free directory listers and new subscribers are not being nurtured toward a paid advertising conversation
Trial/Consult Conversion: advertising inquiries that go cold after the initial form submission have no follow-up sequence
Renewal & Expansion: no apparent emails prompting Bronze advertisers to upgrade or reminding Gold advertisers to renew
Win-Back: no sequence to re-engage unengaged subscribers or lapsed advertisers
toneVoice: Warm, neighborly, and community-proud. Casual but credible — written by locals for locals. Celebratory of small businesses and everyday community members. Conversational 'morning coffee read' energy. No jargon, no corporate speak. Uses 'neighbors' language naturally.
compliance: Standard CAN-SPAM compliance required (unsubscribe link, physical address). No healthcare, finance, or legal vertical constraints. Local Alabama operation — no GDPR complexity expected unless EU subscribers are present. Email contact is a Gmail address, suggesting lean early-stage operation.
